seo.title: LeadCue - Website Prospecting for Agency Outbound
seo.description: LeadCue is a website research layer for agencies: review prospect sites, qualify outreach fit, and prepare source-backed context before CRM or email tools.
seo.structuredDescription: LeadCue helps agencies turn prospect websites into qualified outreach opportunities with fit scores, website evidence, first lines, and CRM-ready context.

hero.eyebrow: Website prospecting for agency outbound teams
hero.subhead: LeadCue helps SEO, web design, and growth agencies review websites, find the reason to contact, and prepare outreach-ready context before accounts move into CRM or email tools.
hero.microcopy: 20 free scans. Google sign-in. No LinkedIn scraping. Built for website review before outreach.
hero.visualNote: Website evidence, fit scoring, and outreach context in one workspace

faqSection.title: Built around website research, not another contact database
faqSection.copy: LeadCue is intentionally narrow: review the company website, qualify the opportunity, and move better context into the outreach workflow you already use.

items.question: Is LeadCue an email finder?
items.answer: No. LeadCue can surface public contact paths from a website, but its main job is to find sales cues, fit signals, outreach angles, and first lines grounded in the pages you choose to analyze.

items.question: Does LeadCue scrape LinkedIn or browse in the background?
items.answer: No. The Chrome extension is designed around user-triggered website analysis. It does not scrape LinkedIn, collect browsing history, or run background prospecting jobs.

items.question: Can I send campaigns from LeadCue?
items.answer: No. LeadCue prepares the research layer before outreach. Save Prospect Cards, copy first lines, or export qualified prospects into your existing tools.

items.question: Who is LeadCue built for first?
items.answer: LeadCue is built first for small SEO, web design, redesign, and growth agencies that do outbound and need specific website-backed reasons to contact prospects.

login.heroTitle: Turn website research into ranked accounts.
login.heroCopy: Source-backed signals, fit scores, and outreach context in one workspace.
login.cardCopy: Use Google or your work email to reopen your website research workspace.
login.resetCopy: Use the same work email attached to the workspace. We will prepare a one-time reset link.
login.resetDevNote: On local builds, LeadCue shows the reset link directly so the flow can be tested end to end.
login.sessionNote: Password sign-in uses the same secure workspace session as Google OAuth.
login.signupCopy: Sign in first. Workspace setup now happens inside /app after your account session is active.

validation.invalidLogin: Enter a valid work email and an 8+ character password.
validation.emailLoginFailed: Email password sign-in failed. Try again.
validation.emailLoginUnavailable: Email password sign-in is unavailable. Try Google or come back in a moment.
validation.invalidResetEmail: Enter the workspace email address so we know where to send the reset link.
validation.resetPrepared: If that email belongs to a workspace, a reset link has been prepared.
validation.resetUnavailable: Password reset is unavailable right now.

reset.title: Choose a new password
reset.copy: This updates the password for your LeadCue workspace login and keeps the same secure session model as Google sign-in.
reset.missingToken: This password reset link is missing its token. Request a new reset from the login page.
reset.shortPassword: Use at least 8 characters for the new password.
reset.mismatch: The new password and confirmation need to match.
reset.invalidLink: This reset link is no longer valid. Request a new one.
reset.genericError: Unable to reset the password right now.

rows.description: The strongest source-backed reason to reach out.

rows.sample: I noticed Northstar explains the product clearly, but the demo path starts after the first scroll.
rows.description: Copy-ready opener based on website evidence.

rows.sample: Homepage: demo CTA appears below the first scroll; navigation scan: no case studies.
rows.description: Evidence that lets reps verify the claim.
Where the account sits before outreach.

firstLine.pickerCopy: Pick a website signal, tailor it to your offer, and copy a reply-ready opener that moves the conversation forward.
firstLine.whyCopy: Lead with an observation the buyer can verify, then connect it to a plausible business outcome.
firstLine.avoidCopy: Generic praise sounds automated and gives the buyer no reason to continue reading.
firstLine.badExample: Loved your site and thought I'd reach out because we help companies grow.

templates.firstLine: I noticed the product story is clear, but the main demo path does not show up until after the first scroll.
templates.nextSentence: That can cost booked demos from visitors who already understand what you do.
templates.cta: Want me to send over three above-the-fold ideas?

templates.firstLine: I saw a strong product explanation, but buyer proof and case studies are hard to find from the main navigation.
templates.nextSentence: For higher-consideration buyers, that can make the demo decision feel riskier than it needs to be.
templates.cta: Want me to send a quick proof-section outline?

templates.firstLine: I noticed the blog appears inactive, even though the product seems to serve buyers who research before booking a demo.
templates.nextSentence: That may leave high-intent educational searches uncovered.
templates.cta: Want me to send a few content angles I would test first?

templates.firstLine: I noticed the homepage names the product category, but the buyer-specific outcome takes a few sections to become clear.
templates.nextSentence: Tightening that message can help visitors understand why the product is relevant faster.
templates.cta: Want me to send two headline directions?

seo.seoNextSentence: That usually means high-intent search demand is leaking before buyers ever make it to a demo request.
seo.defaultNextSentence: That can hide demand capture opportunities when buyers research through search before they ever talk to sales.
seo.seoCta: Want me to send the two search-driven fixes I would test first?
seo.defaultCta: Want me to send the SEO angle I would use here first?
seo.why: It ties a visible website issue to search demand and makes the next step feel lightweight.

marketing.trustNextSentence: That can make paid and outbound traffic bounce before they feel enough confidence to reply or book.
marketing.defaultNextSentence: That usually creates friction between the click and the action you want a buyer to take.
marketing.cta: Want me to send the conversion angle I would test first?
marketing.why: It connects the website cue to funnel friction and frames the reply as a small review, not a pitch dump.

web_design.why: It names a real website observation, connects it to buyer behavior, and ends with a low-pressure ask.

checklist.copy: Check the signals you can verify on a prospect website before saving, exporting, or assigning the account to outreach.
checklist.nextStepReady: save the lead and draft the first line
checklist.nextStepMore: capture more website evidence before export

items.label: Primary CTA is visible above the fold.
Relevant customer proof appears before the buyer must hunt for it.
Case studies or outcomes are accessible from navigation.
Blog, resources, or service content looks current.
Service or use-case pages answer specific buyer questions.
The headline explains the target buyer and outcome clearly.
Contact, demo, or booking path is easy to find.
The strongest observation can be tied to a page source.

integration.title: __PLATFORM__ CSV header and sample row
integration.copy: Use these headers as a starting point for selected saved leads from a website-first research workflow.
integration.quickWinsTitle: What makes this import immediately useful

quickWins: Map Fit score and Confidence score into custom company properties.
Keep First line and Source notes visible to SDRs as soon as the record lands.
Import only selected saved leads so list hygiene stays clean.
mistakes: Importing every scanned account instead of only saved leads.
Dropping source notes so reps lose the proof behind the opener.
Using contact import too early before a verified contact path exists.

quickWins: Set Lead Source to Website Prospecting or LeadCue for cleaner reporting.
Use Description or a long-text custom field for source-backed summary.
Route owner and lead status before the campaign handoff.
mistakes: Over-mapping every research field into a rigid schema.
Skipping custom numeric fields for fit and confidence scores.
Moving accounts into sequences before the owner verifies the reason to reach out.

quickWins: Use Organization fields for website identity and note fields for the research reason.
Map Stage to researching or qualified before the account is touched by outreach.
Keep owner attached so handoff stays accountable.
mistakes: Creating deals before the account has shown any engagement.
Burying source notes in a place reps never review.
Losing export status and re-importing the same account twice.
